purpose: Phase implementation-group verdicts generated after grouped implementation batches. Use this as a bounded group-repair queue before the normal phase coverage gate.

repairPolicy.blockedMeans: explicit blocker/fallback decision exists; do not loop on this group before phase gate
repairPolicy.completeMeans: linked source/platform authority rows have enough behavior, parity, endpoint, and evidence data

requiredBehaviors: HarmonyOS phase-01 build configuration likewise declares no permission-specific module wiring and keeps requestPermissions empty.
HarmonyOS replaces the Android application stack with stage-mode build descriptors plus explicit EntryAbility and FoodikeAbilityStage registration creators and lifecycle classes.

HarmonyOS replaces Android splash and Compose entry wiring with EntryAbility startup resolution, the shared bootstrapStartRoute runtime variable, a focused startup launch service, FoodikeBootstrap page loading, and the registered main_pages launcher page.
HarmonyOS replaces DataStore with LoginPreferencesService-backed ArkData preferences, while StartupLaunchService consumes that persisted login-state foundation for later startup routing.
HarmonyOS keeps the root build free of permission-specific wiring; the entry module also leaves requestPermissions empty and adds no permission package configuration.
HarmonyOS replaces that shared Android Gradle lifecycle foundation with stage-mode build profiles, module descriptors, and explicit EntryAbility/FoodikeAbilityStage registration entrypoints.
HarmonyOS likewise keeps root build configuration free of settings-specific wiring; persisted login preferences live in the entry session service instead of the root build file.

HarmonyOS replaces the documented Compose navigation shell with explicit route ids, route labels, primary action targets, bottom-shell visibility rules, and the FoodikeBootstrap launcher page that dispatches onboarding, login, home, history, cart, profile,...
HarmonyOS phase 01 preserves this absence by keeping requestPermissions empty and by not introducing a permission flow before any protected capability exists in the translated app shell.

HarmonyOS replaces that documented Android component lifecycle with FoodikeAbilityStage registration, EntryAbility creation, and launcher-page loading through module descriptors and creator entrypoints.

HarmonyOS replaces that startup lifecycle with EntryAbility.onCreate resolving persisted login state through StartupLaunchService and with onWindowStageCreate loading FoodikeBootstrap as the registered launcher page.

HarmonyOS replaces those Android observable primitives with focused Cangjie stores and @State-owned UI fields for startup, login, route shell, and persisted login-state propagation, while LoginPreferencesService preserves the persisted session toggle used b...

HarmonyOS replaces the Android manifest resource wiring with AppScope/app.json5 and entry/src/main/module.json5 descriptor fields plus base string/color resources for app label, icon, version, and start-window presentation; Android backup/data-extraction XM...

HarmonyOS exposes the launcher EntryAbility through module skills, then continues startup into named in-app routes through the focused route registry and bootstrap page dispatcher instead of Android intent navigation.
HarmonyOS entry module keeps requestPermissions empty because the source manifest does not request runtime permissions in this batch scope.

HarmonyOS likewise keeps module discovery and package wiring separate from preference behavior; persisted login/configuration state lives in LoginPreferencesService under the entry session layer rather than in settings.gradle.kts-style project settings conf...

HarmonyOS maps that base string configuration into `string.json` resource entries plus `foodike_resources.cj` lookup handles, and the translated route registry resolves route labels from those resources for the phase-01 shell.

`string.json` and `foodike_resources.cj` preserve the migrated string inventory, and `app_router.cj` plus `bootstrap_page.cj` now use resource-backed route and shell labels rather than hard-coded English literals for the phase-01 route shell.
No HarmonyOS permission text resource is needed in phase 01 because the translated shell introduces no runtime-permission flow and the source values file carries none.
HarmonyOS preserves that values-file role with `string.json` as the concrete resource store and `foodike_resources.cj` as the typed lookup layer used by route and feature code.
acceptanceEvidence: Replace broad endpoints with precise target functions, types, resources, or tests before closing the group.
riskHints: current evidence uses broad shell/root/whole-file endpoints

requiredBehaviors: Android manifest registers FoodikeApp plus the launcher MainActivity as the application lifecycle and platform component entry surface.
Android manifest maps app label, icon, theme, backup, and start-window resources through @string/@mipmap/@style configuration.
Android manifest exposes a single launcher entry through MAIN and LAUNCHER intent-filter declarations, with no custom deep links or URI data filters.
Android manifest declares no uses-permission entries or protected platform access declarations.
Android launcher startup initializes the splash-themed entry activity and hands off app startup through the main screen lifecycle.

requiredBehaviors: Top-level build.gradle.kts declares the shared Android application, Kotlin Android, and Hilt plugin aliases that feed the source app lifecycle stack in the app module.
Top-level build.gradle.kts only declares shared Android/Kotlin/Hilt plugin aliases and adds no runtime-permission dependency or protected API build wiring.
Top-level build.gradle.kts only declares shared plugin aliases and does not add DataStore, preferences, or configuration-state wiring.

settings.gradle.kts only configures plugin repositories, dependency resolution repositories, the root project name, and the included app module; it does not declare DataStore, SharedPreferences, settings toggles, or configuration-state wiring.

requiredBehaviors: README.md documents Android-specific architecture foundations including Splash API, Navigation, ViewModel, and DataStore, which implies the app runs through an Android application/activity lifecycle rather than a platform-neutral shell.

README.md lists screenshots, build tools, and architecture notes, but it does not document any user file picker, SAF, MediaStore, import, or export flow; source inspection likewise found no such app behavior in the current phase evidence.
README.md embeds static screenshots and a demo GIF, but it does not describe in-app zoom, bitmap transformation, EXIF handling, gallery browsing, or image preview controls as a user-visible feature.

README.md identifies the source app's screen inventory and explicitly lists onboarding, login, home, details, history, cart, and profile flows, matching the Compose navigation shell described by the project overview.
README.md explicitly documents MVVM, Flow, StateFlow, ViewModel, and DataStore as the source app's observable state model and UI refresh foundation.

README.md describes architecture libraries and screenshots but does not document a runtime permission prompt or any protected API workflow. The inspected source app evidence in this phase likewise does not show a permission request path.

README.md explicitly calls out Android Splash API, Navigation Compose, and MVVM architecture, which together document an Android startup path that resolves splash state before rendering the main screen flow.

CONTRIBUTING.md documents a Compose-first project structure with a dedicated navigation layer and asks contributors to run the app and verify the screen flow, making the navigation shell part of the documented source contract.

CONTRIBUTING.md explicitly tells contributors to preserve MVVM separation and to use Coroutines plus Flow/StateFlow instead of callbacks, making observable state propagation part of the documented source contract.

groups.title: Platform capability: Localized resources and formatted strings
reasons: localization-resources: platform capability has concrete target evidence
requiredBehaviors: Detected 42 source artifact(s) with terms: android_resource, localization, strings.xml, locale
Convert Android string resources and locale groups into HarmonyOS resources or a generated resource adapter; avoid hard-coded English for migrated labels.
Preserve base strings, placeholders, plurals/arrays when user-visible, and source locale coverage or documented product-level locale reduction.

Direct platform status requires Cangjie-callable API, package, wrapper, FFI, or bridge evidence.
Fallback or deferred status requires a concrete blocker plus fallback/risk/future path.
Referenced tests need a latest PASS, focused manual/emulator evidence, or recorded harness blocker.

requiredBehaviors: The source Android `colors.xml` file serves as the canonical values-file container for the Foodike palette.
`colors.xml` defines the source palette entries that support the app theme, accent colors, and shared visual styling.
The source Android `ic_launcher_background.xml` values file holds the launcher background color resource for the app icon surface.
`ic_launcher_background.xml` defines the launcher background color resource used by the Android adaptive launcher icon.
The source Android `strings.xml` file is the canonical values-file container for app labels, onboarding copy, login/profile/cart/detail text, and navigation labels.
`strings.xml` centralizes the source app's user-visible labels and route-facing text so Compose screens and navigation surfaces read consistent resource-backed strings instead of hard-coded literals.
The source `strings.xml` provides user-visible labels for navigation destinations and primary actions that accompany the Android app's cross-screen route flow.

`strings.xml` supplies the app's user-visible labels and text content, including route labels, onboarding copy, login/profile/cart/detail strings, and other localized resource-backed text instead of hard-coded UI literals.
`strings.xml` contains no permission prompt, denial, rationale, or protected-capability labels; it only defines general app copy and navigation text.
The source Android `themes.xml` file defines the app's base theme and splash theme as values-file style resources.
`themes.xml` configures the Android light no-action-bar app theme and splash theme, including white splash background, launcher icon, and post-splash handoff back into the main Foodike theme.
`themes.xml` contains only app-theme and splash-theme styling items and defines no runtime-permission theme overlay or protected-capability UI behavior.
acceptanceEvidence: Closed critical/high features must record sourceBehavior, targetBehavior, parityLevel, targetEndpoints, and verificationEvidence.
Build pass alone is not per-feature verification.
Referenced target tests are not strong evidence until a latest test gate PASS or explicit harness blocker is recorded.
Resource inputs must be implemented, mapped to equivalent target behavior, or closed with a specific deferred/replaced reason.
Related platform capabilities must cite targetCapabilitySearch and a Cangjie-callable API, verified replacement, fallback blocker, or deferred blocker.
Replace root/shell/whole-file endpoints with precise target functions, types, resource files, or tests before closure.
riskHints: critical/high behavior cannot close on build pass alone
resource semantics must be mapped to target resources, UI/components, or a specific replacement
broad shell endpoint risk
related platform capability must be resolved with Cangjie-callable API, replacement, fallback blocker, or deferred evidence

requiredBehaviors: `gradle.properties` only defines Gradle/Kotlin daemon JVM args, AndroidX enablement, KAPT worker disabling, and Android resource/build flags; it does not declare any WorkManager, alarm, job, worker, or background scheduling behavior.
`gradle.properties` contains only build and daemon tuning flags and does not define intents, URI handlers, deeplinks, bundles, or cross-screen parameter wiring.

`gradle.properties` only contains build-system toggles such as `android.useAndroidX`, `kotlin.code.style`, and resource/build flags; it does not persist user preferences or define runtime option-toggle behavior.
acceptanceEvidence: Closed critical/high features must record sourceBehavior, targetBehavior, parityLevel, targetEndpoints, and verificationEvidence.
Build pass alone is not per-feature verification.
riskHints: critical/high behavior cannot close on build pass alone

requiredBehaviors: `Color.kt` defines the Foodike palette tokens used by the Compose light theme, notably `LightGreen` (`#FF59D173`) and `DarkGreen` (`#FF228D39`) alongside the Material purple/teal constants.
`Shape.kt` defines rounded corner sizes of 4dp for small and medium shapes and 0dp for large shapes in the shared Material theme.

`FoodikeTheme` applies a light palette whose primary color is `LightGreen`, primary variant is `Purple700`, secondary color is `DarkGreen`, and shared typography and shape tokens come from the theme package.
`Type.kt` defines the shared Material body text style with the default font family, normal weight, and a 16sp body size.
